sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used throughout the National Identity Card Act, including biometric information, digital wallet, identity card, Minister, Mobile ID, MNIC System, NIC number, register, Registrar, and Unit. These definitions establish the scope and meaning of critical concepts for the implementation of the identity card system and related processes.
sections.source_quote: 2. Interpretation In this Act— "biometric information", in relation to an individual, means data about his external characteristics, including his fingerprints; "digital wallet" means a digital application issued by the National Identity Card Unit to store such digital documents as may be prescribed; "identity card" means a card issued under section 5(1); "Minister" means the Minister to whom responsiblity for the subject of civil status is assigned; "Mobile ID" means the digital identity document which the holder of an identity card may register under section 5(2B) and which is stored in the digital wallet; "MNIC System" means the hardware, software and infrastructure for the registration, personalisation, issuance and post-issuance of the identity card and Mobile ID; "NIC number" has the same meaning as in the Civil Status Act; "register" means the register kept under section 3; "Registrar" means the Registrar of Civil Status; "Unit" means the National Identity Card Unit referred to in section 2A.

sections.heading: Designation of office
sections.summary: This section states that data collection and processing under the Act must comply with the Data Protection Act, establishing the legal framework for handling personal information. It ensures that all data activities are subject to privacy protections.

requirements.text: The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act.

sections.source_quote: 12. Collection and processing of data to be subject to Data Protection Act The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act.

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.summary: This section defines key terms used throughout the National Identity Card Act, including biometric information, digital wallet, identity card, Minister, Mobile ID, MNIC System, NIC number, register, Registrar, and Unit. These definitions establish the scope and meaning of critical concepts for the implementation of the identity card system and related processes.
sections.source_quote: 2. Interpretation In this Act— "biometric information", in relation to an individual, means data about his external characteristics, including his fingerprints; "digital wallet" means a digital application issued by the National Identity Card Unit to store such digital documents as may be prescribed; "identity card" means a card issued under section 5(1); "Minister" means the Minister to whom responsiblity for the subject of civil status is assigned; "Mobile ID" means the digital identity document which the holder of an identity card may register under section 5(2B) and which is stored in the digital wallet; "MNIC System" means the hardware, software and infrastructure for the registration, personalisation, issuance and post-issuance of the identity card and Mobile ID; "NIC number" has the same meaning as in the Civil Status Act; "register" means the register kept under section 3; "Registrar" means the Registrar of Civil Status; "Unit" means the National Identity Card Unit referred to in section 2A.

sections.heading: Duty to be registered
sections.summary: This section mandates that citizens of Mauritius must apply for an identity card within six months of turning 18, with exceptions for non-residents or those with serious illnesses. Applicants must provide birth certificates, other documents, and biometric information, with medical certifications allowing exemptions for those unable to provide biometrics due to permanent disabilities.

requirements.text: Every person who is a citizen of Mauritius shall, within 6 months of attaining the age of 18 and on such form as may be prescribed, apply for an identity card at any office designated by the Head of the Unit.

Where a person is not a resident of Mauritius or is unable to comply with paragraph (a) on account of serious illness, as certified by a medical practitioner in the public service, or on any other compelling and reasonable ground, he may apply for an identity card in such manner and within such period of attaining the age of 18 as the Head of the Unit may determine.
Every person who applies for an identity card shall produce his birth certificate or his certificate of registration or naturalisation as a citizen of Mauritius, as the case may be.
Every person who applies for an identity card shall produce such other documents as the Head of the Unit may require.
Every person who applies for an identity card shall allow his fingerprints, and other biometric information about himself, to be taken and recorded.
Every person who applies for an identity card shall allow himself to be photographed, for the purpose of the identity card.

Subsection (2)(c) shall not apply to a person who is certified by a medical practitioner in the public service to be unable to provide his biometric information by reason of any permanent infirmity or permanent disability.

sections.source_quote: 4. Duty to be registered (1) (a) Subject to paragraph (b), every person who is a citizen of Mauritius shall, within 6 months of attaining the age of 18 and on such form as may be prescribed, apply for an identity card at any office designated by the Head of the Unit. (b) Where a person – (i) is not a resident of Mauritius; or (ii) is unable to comply with paragraph (a) on account of serious illness, as certified by a medical practitioner in the public service, or on any other compelling and reasonable ground, he may apply for an identity card in such manner and within such period of attaining the age of 18 as the Head of the Unit may determine. (2) Every person who applies for an identity card shall — (a) produce his birth certificate or his certificate of registration or naturalisation as a citizen of Mauritius, as the case may be; (b) produce such other documents as the Head of the Unit may require; (c) allow his fingerprints, and other biornetric information about himself, to be taken and recorded; and (d) allow himself to be photographed, for the purpose of the identity card.

sections.heading: Change of residence
sections.summary: This section requires individuals who have obtained an identity card to notify the National Identity Card Unit within 28 days of changing their residence. The notification must be submitted in a prescribed form, ensuring timely updates to the identity card system.

requirements.text: Where any person issued with an identity card changes his residence, he shall, within 28 days of the occurrence, notify the change at any office designated by the Head of the Unit or in such manner as may be prescribed.
Any change of residence reported under subsection (1) shall be in such form as may be prescribed.

sections.source_quote: 6. Change of residence (1) Where any person issued with an identity card changes his residence, he shall, within 28 days of the occurrence, notify the change at any office designated by the Head of the Unit or in such manner as may be prescribed.

sections.heading: Collection and processing of data to be subject to Data Protection Act
sections.summary: This section explicitly states that all personal data, including biometric information, collected and processed under the National Identity Card Act must comply with the Data Protection Act, ensuring that data handling adheres to privacy regulations.

requirements.text: The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act.

sections.source_quote: 12. Collection and processing of data to be subject to Data Protection Act The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act.
